close_reason: Style: .ok() vs let _ = for Result discard — both are idiomatic
description: secretx-k8s/src/lib.rs:402, 589. backend.client.set(client).ok(); converts Result<(), Client> to Option<()> and drops it. Clippy-level idiom: 'let _ = expr;' is the conventional way to silently discard a Result in Rust. .ok() is for converting Result to Option when you want the Option for further chaining. Filing as opinion because both work; this is taste with no engineering basis.
title: .client.set(client).ok() should be 'let _ = backend.client.set(client);' for Result discard
